This is the set of background threads that actually perform the blocking operations.</font></li><li><font style="font-size: 36px;"><b>An Internal Job Queue:&nbsp;</b></font><font style=""><span style="font-size: 36px;">The Thread Pool has its </span><b style="font-size: 36px;">own internal, private queue</b><span style="font-size: 36px;"> to manage the jobs assigned to it. It's a To-Do list for</span><span style="background-color: rgb(204, 255, 153);"><b style=""><font style="font-size: 42px;">&nbsp;'👷🏼<u style="">worker threads</u>'.</font></b></span></font></li><li><font style=""><b style=""><span style="font-size: 36px;">A Synchronization Mechanism 🤝:&nbsp;</span><font style="font-size: value="<font style="font-size: 60px;"><h3 data-path-to-node="3" style=""><b data-path-to-node="3" data-index-in-node="0" style="">1. JavaScript Engine (The Core Execution Unit)</b></h3><h3 data-path-to-node="2" style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, sans-serif !important; line-height: 1.15 !important; margin-top: 0px !important;"><p data-path-to-node="4" id="p-rc_fe99877238e4e83c-335" style=""><span data-path-to-node="4,0">Even without a browser, the engine remains the heart of the runtime</span><span data-path-to-node="4,1"><sup class="superscript"></sup></span><span data-path-to-node="4,2">.</span></p><div class="source-inline-chip-container ng-star-inserted" style=""></div><ul data-path-to-node="5" style=""><li style=""><p data-path-to-node="5,0,0" id="p-rc_fe99877238e4e83c-336"><span data-path-to-node="5,0,0,0"><b data-path-to-node="5,0,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">Role:</b> Compiles and executes JavaScript code into machine instructions</span><span data-path-to-node="5,0,0,1"><sup class="superscript"></sup></span><span data-path-to-node="5,0,0,2">.</span></p><div class="source-inline-chip-container ng-star-inserted"></div></li><li style=""><p data-path-to-node="5,1,0" id="p-rc_fe99877238e4e83c-337" style=""><span data-path-to-node="5,1,0,0" style=""><b data-path-to-node="5,1,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">Components:</b> For Node/Deno, it is <b data-path-to-node="5,1,0,0" data-index-in-node="33">V8</b> (featuring the <b data-path-to-node="5,1,0,0" data-index-in-node="51">Ignition</b> interpreter and <b data-path-to-node="5,1,0,0" data-index-in-node="76">TurboFan</b> compiler); for Bun, it is <b data-path-to-node="5,1,0,0" data-index-in-node="111">JavaScriptCore</b></span><span data-path-to-node="5,1,0,1" style=""><sup class="superscript"></sup></span><span data-path-to-node="5,1,0,2" style="">.</span></p></li></ul></h3></font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="1260" width="1816.2" x="144" y="600" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-653" parent="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-644" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=#36393d;fillColor=#ffff88;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="<font style="font-size: 60px;"><h3 data-path-to-node="6" style=""><b data-path-to-node="6" data-index-in-node="0" style="">2. Runtime APIs / Native Bindings (The OS Bridge)</b></h3><h3 data-path-to-node="3" style=""><p data-path-to-node="7" id="p-rc_fe99877238e4e83c-338" style=""><span data-path-to-node="7,0">Since there is no <code data-path-to-node="7,0" data-index-in-node="18">window</code> or <code data-path-to-node="7,0" data-index-in-node="28">document</code> object, the runtime provides APIs to interact with the server's environment</span><span data-path-to-node="7,1"><sup class="superscript"></sup></span><span data-path-to-node="7,2">.</span> </p><div class="source-inline-chip-container ng-star-inserted" style=""></div><ul data-path-to-node="8" style=""><li style=""><p data-path-to-node="8,0,0" id="p-rc_fe99877238e4e83c-339"><span data-path-to-node="8,0,0,0"><b data-path-to-node="8,0,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">File System (fs):</b> Allows reading, writing, and managing files on the hard drive</span><span data-path-to-node="8,0,0,1"><sup class="superscript"></sup></span><span data-path-to-node="8,0,0,2">.</span> </p><div class="source-inline-chip-container ng-star-inserted"></div></li><li style=""><p data-path-to-node="8,1,0" id="p-rc_fe99877238e4e83c-340"><span data-path-to-node="8,1,0,0"><b data-path-to-node="8,1,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">Network Stack (http/https/net):</b> Enables the creation of web servers and handling of TCP/UDP sockets</span><span data-path-to-node="8,1,0,1"><sup class="superscript"></sup></span><span data-path-to-node="8,1,0,2">.</span></p><div class="source-inline-chip-container ng-star-inserted"></div></li><li style=""><p data-path-to-node="8,2,0,1"><sup class="superscript"></sup></span><span data-path-to-node="8,2,0,2">.</span> </p><div class="source-inline-chip-container ng-star-inserted"></div></li><li style=""><p high-level security/encryption and system-level information (CPU, RAM usage)</span><span data-path-to-node="8,3,0,1" style=""><sup class="superscript"></sup></span><span data-path-to-node="8,3,0,2" value="<font style="font-size: 60px;"><h3 data-path-to-node="12"><b data-path-to-node="12" data-index-in-node="0">4. Native Addon API (N-API / FFI)</b></h3><h3 data-path-to-node="3"><p data-path-to-node="13" id="p-rc_fe99877238e4e83c-346"><span data-path-to-node="13,0">This layer allows JavaScript to communicate with low-level C, C++, or Rust code</span><span data-path-to-node="13,1"><sup class="superscript"></sup></span><span data-path-to-node="13,2">.</span> </p><div class="source-inline-chip-container ng-star-inserted"></div><ul data-path-to-node="14"><li><p data-path-to-node="14,0,0" id="p-rc_fe99877238e4e83c-347"><span data-path-to-node="14,0,0,0"><b data-path-to-node="14,0,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">N-API (Node-API):</b> Provides a stable interface for C++ addons to ensure they don't break when the JS engine updates</span><span data-path-to-node="14,0,0,1"><sup class="superscript"></sup></span><span data-path-to-node="14,0,0,2">.</span> </p><div class="source-inline-chip-container ng-star-inserted"></div></li><li><p data-path-to-node="14,1,0" id="p-rc_fe99877238e4e83c-348"><span data-path-to-node="14,1,0,0"><b data-path-to-node="14,1,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">FFI (Foreign Function Interface):</b> Used heavily in Bun (Bun-ffi) to call native functions with near-zero overhead</span><span data-path-to-node="14,1,0,1"><sup class="superscript"></sup></span><span data-path-to-node="14,1,0,2">.</span></p></li></ul></h3></font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="1310" width="2040" x="4660" y="2205" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-655" parent="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-644" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=#36393d;fillColor=#cdeb8b;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="<font style="font-size: 60px;"><h3 data-path-to-node="9"><b data-path-to-node="9" data-index-in-node="0">3. Event Loop &amp; Thread Pool (The Asynchronous Orchestrator)</b></h3><h3 data-path-to-node="3"><p data-path-to-node="10" id="p-rc_fe99877238e4e83c-343"><span data-path-to-node="10,0">Server runtimes value="<font style=""><span style="font-weight: bold; font-size: 72px;">Hot Code/Hot Function:</span><br><font style="font-size: 36px;">Those codes <b>Loops,</b>&nbsp;<b style="">func()</b> or <b style="">func(loop)</b> which calls, <b style="">loops(not untile loop end)</b> &amp; run multiple times. Interpreter generates byte code and side by side <b style="">counting in backgound by Profiling counter</b>&nbsp;&amp; also <b style="">detects in loop's internal code(On-Stack Replacement-OSR). It's also used in <span style="background-color: rgb(229, 204, 255);">Hidden Class inline Caching.</span></b></font></font><div><span style="font-size: 36px;"><b>--------------------------------------------------------------------------<br></b></span><div><font style=""><font style=""><font style="font-size: 36px; background-color: rgb(255, 102, 102);">"<b style="">Hot Code</b> detection is the <b style="">'Brain' of V8</b>. By using <b style="">Profiling counters and OSR</b>, the <b>V8::platform TaskScheduler</b>&nbsp;identifies repetitive paths and offloads them to <b>background compiler threads (V8 pick Hot Code from RAM &amp; put into the CPU Registers)</b>. This ensures the<b style=""> main thread is never blocked </b>while the JIT pipeline transforms slow <b>Bytecode</b> into high-performance <b>Machine Code.</b>"</font><span style="font-size: style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;gradientColor=#99FF99;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="<br><div><font style="font-size: 100px;"><b>"V8 maps JS variables to CPU Registers(CPU's internal registers) for near-instant calculations(using ALU) &amp; also holds the result(in ALU registers)."</b><b style=""></b></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 100px;"><b style="">CPU Register for Pointer Management: </b>Its holds the CallStack's and HEAP's address in special registers.</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 100px;"><b>Control Registers(RSP+RBP):&nbsp;</b></font><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><font style="font-size: 100px;">Holds Execution Context Addresses.</font></span></div><div><br></div><div><font style="font-size: 100px;"><b><font style="color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">1. Register Stack Pointer(RSP):</font> </b>It tells which func() is running[current execution context]</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 100px;"><b><font style="color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">2. Register Base Pointer(RBP):</font></b> It track the starting points of func() variables. First RSP after execution stored as RBP</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 100px;">Without RSP&amp;RBP registers JS can't know where GEC ends and from where FEC starts.</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 100px;"><b>ALU Rgister(RAX): </b>It's hold func(){return value} and ALU OP results.</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 100px;"><font style="font-weight: bold; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">1. Register Accumulator X(RAX)</font><font style="color: rgb(153, 0, 0);"><b>[64-bit]:</b></font><font style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">&nbsp;It's holds the func(){return} value &amp; ALU's operational result.</font></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 100px;"><b>CPU Internal Registers= General Purpose Registers(Takes Data from RAM &amp; Holds in Variable Registers)+ALU Registers(RAX)(Holds ALU's compute Result)+Control Registers(RSP+RBP)</b></font></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="1625.28" width="7230" y="524.72" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1008" parent="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1006" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;arcSize=50;gradientColor=#66FFFF;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="<font style=""><b style=""><span style="font-size: 200px;">CPU Registers</span><font style="font-size: 100px;">(Fastest Storage)</font><span style="font-size: 200px;"> &amp; V8</span></b></font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="295.33" width="5256.53" x="853.47" y="310" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1009" parent="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=#d79b00;fillColor=#ffe6cc;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="<div><font style="font-size: 100px;"><b style="">Thread Concepts:</b></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);"><b>Definition:</b></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">"A thread is the smallest unit of execution&nbsp;</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">within a process. It represents a sequence&nbsp;</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">of instructions that can be managed&nbsp;</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">independently by the OS scheduler."</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(0, 0, 153);"><b>Elements of a Thread:</b></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ Thread ID (Unique identifier)</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ Program Counter (Current instruction)</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ Register Set (CPU registers state)</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ Stack (Local variables, call stack)</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">└─ Thread State (Running, Ready, Blocked)</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(0, 102, 0);"><b style="">Types of Threads:</b></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ User-Level / Logical/ Software Threads (Managed by runtime)</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ Kernel-Level/ Native/ OS Threads (Managed by OS)</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ Hardware Threads (Physical CPU cores)</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">└─ Hybrid Threads (M:N threading model)</font></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="1780" width="2100" x="21380" y="8170" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1010" parent="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=none;fillColor=#E5CCFF;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;gradientColor=#CCE5FF;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="<div><font style="font-size: 100px;"><b>V8 Thread Family:</b></font></div><div><br></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);"><b>Main Thread:</b></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ JavaScript Execution</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ Garbage Collection (partial)</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">└─ Event Loop Processing</font></div><div><br></div><div><font style="color: rgb(0, 102, 0);"><br></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(0, 102, 0);"><b>Background Threads:</b></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ Compiler Threads (TurboFan optimization)</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ GC Threads (Concurrent marking/sweeping)</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ WASM Compiler Threads</font></div><div><span style="font-size: 72px;">└─ Profiler Threads</span></div><div><br></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(0, 0, 204);"><b>Helper Threads:</b></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ Snapshot Creation</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">├─ Code Cache Writing</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 72px;">└─ Symbol Table Management</font></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="1630" width="1910" x="23890" y="8270" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1011" parent="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=#82b366;fillColor=#d5e8d4;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="<font style="font-size: 100px;"><b style="">Threads Elements Breakdown:</b></font><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b style="">1. Private Elements</b>(Thread Specific):<b style="">&nbsp;</b></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;i.Program Counter: </span><font style="">Threads Compass</font></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b style="">ii. Stack: </b><font style="">Thread's Memory</font></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">iii. Registers: </span><font style="">RSP,RBP, ALU resgiters(RAX)</font></font></div><div><font style=""><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b style="">iv. THread ID(TID): </b><font style="">Unique <b style="">ID</b> given by <b style="">OS</b>.</font></font></font></div><div><font style=""><font style=""><font style="font-size: 60px;">-----------------------------------------------------------------------</font></font></font></div><div><font style=""><font style=""><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b style="">2. Shared Components</b>(Process-Level):</font></font></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b style="">i. HEAP Memory Access: </b><font style="">Main Thread+GC Thread+Compiler Thread all access this HEAP for processing.</font></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b style="">ii. Code Segment: </b><font style="">That<b style="">&nbsp;</b>Machine code run by CPU.</font></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b>iii.Static/Global Variable:</b>&nbsp;<font style="">Same for the whole Program.</font></font></div><div><span style="font-size: 72px;">-----------------------------------------------------------</span></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b style="">3.Logical Elements</b>(Working Priciples)<b style="">:</b></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b>i. Scheduling Priority:&nbsp;</b>&nbsp;<font style="">OS Scheduler gives priority(High/Low) to every threads, processed in background simultanously.</font></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b style="">ii.State: </b><font style="">Defines the States of Threads.</font></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b style="">&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;(i) Running: </b><font style="">Execution is running on CPU.</font></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b style="">&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; (ii)Ready: </b><font style="">Task is<b style="">&nbsp;</b>Ready for CPU.</font></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><b style="">&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; (iii)Waiting/Blocked: </b><font style="">I/O task waiting for complition.</font></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;">-----------------------------------------------------------------------</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 60px;"><font style=""><b style="">4.Context Switch: </b><font style="">RSP converts into RBP.&nbsp;</font></font><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">In This Proccess OS save thread's component(Registers, PC)</span></font><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); font-size: 60px;">&nbsp;</span></div><div><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); font-size: 60px;">-----------------------------------------------------------------------</span></div><div><b style=""><font style="font-size: 72px;">5. Carrier Mapping(The Bridge):</font></b><span style="font-size: 60px;"> It is a carrier to run on CPU.</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 60px;"><b>i. M:N Mapping: </b>Map&nbsp;"M" no. of logical threads on "N" no. of Native(Kernal) Threads.</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 60px;"><b>ii.Worker Threads: </b>Node.js , Bun, Deno makes "worker logical thread" which uses runTime native threadPool.</span></div><div><font style=""><font style=""><font style="font-size: 72px;"><br></font></font></font></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="4000" width="1080" x="25990" y="5750" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1012" parent="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=none;fillColor=default;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;gradientColor=#99FFCC;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="<h3 data-path-to-node="4"><font style="font-size: 100px;">Threads Working Proccess:</font></h3><h3 data-path-to-node="4"><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">1. The Context Packet (The State)</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="5"><font style="font-size: 72px;">The most significant element of a logical thread is its <b data-path-to-node="5" data-index-in-node="56">State</b>. When a thread is paused (during a context switch), its current state is saved.</font></p><ul data-path-to-node="6"><li><p data-path-to-node="6,0,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="6,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">Instruction Pointer:</b> This keeps track of how much code the Logical Thread has executed.</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="6,1,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="6,1,0" data-index-in-node="0">Registers State:</b> Even though physical registers exist within the CPU, every logical thread keeps a "Copy" (Snapshot) of its registers in memory. This ensures that when it returns, it can resume exactly from where it left off.</font></p></li></ul><h3 data-path-to-node="7"><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">2. Thread Stack (The Private Memory)</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="8"><font style="font-size: 72px;">Every logical thread has its own virtual memory segment called the <b data-path-to-node="8" data-index-in-node="67">Stack</b>.</font></p><ul data-path-to-node="9"><li><p data-path-to-node="9,0,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="9,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">Local Variables:</b> Temporary variables created inside functions.</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="9,1,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="9,1,0" data-index-in-node="0">Function Call History:</b> A record of which function called whom (Call Stack).</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="9,2,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="9,2,0" data-index-in-node="0">Logic:</b> Since this is at a logical level, the size of this stack is decided by the runtime (Node/Bun/Deno), such as a default of 1MB.</font></p></li></ul><h3 data-path-to-node="10"><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">3. Scheduling Metadata (The Manager)</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="11"><font style="font-size: 72px;">Since a single process can have thousands of logical threads, certain elements are necessary to manage them:</font></p><ul data-path-to-node="12"><li><p data-path-to-node="12,0,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="12,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">Priority Level:</b> Determines which thread is more important (e.g., UI thread vs. Background thread).</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="12,1,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="12,1,0" data-index-in-node="0">Thread ID (LID):</b> A logical identity used by the runtime (which may differ from the Native TID).</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="12,2,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="12,2,0" data-index-in-node="0">Status:</b> Defines the current phase (Ready, Running, Blocked, Waiting).</font></p></li></ul><h3 data-path-to-node="13"><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">4. The Carrier Mapping (The Bridge)</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="14"><font style="font-size: 72px;">A logical thread cannot run on hardware by itself; it requires a <b data-path-to-node="14" data-index-in-node="65">Carrier</b>.</font></p><ul data-path-to-node="15"><li><p data-path-to-node="15,0,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="15,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">M:N Mapping:</b> This refers to mapping "M" number of Logical Threads onto "N" number of Native (Kernel) Threads.</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="15,1,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="15,1,0" data-index-in-node="0">Worker Threads:</b> In Node.js or Bun, when you create a "Worker," it acts as a logical thread that utilizes the runtime's native thread pool.</font></p></li></ul>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="2270" width="4380" x="21340" y="5680" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1013" parent="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=none;fillColor=default;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;gradientColor=#999999;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="<h3 data-path-to-node="2"><font style="font-size: 150px;">Types OF APIs</font></h3><h3 data-path-to-node="2"><font style="font-size: 72px;">1. Networking &amp; Communication APIs</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="3"><font style="font-size: 72px;">Yeh APIs external servers aur devices se data exchange karne ka kaam karti hain.</font></p><ul data-path-to-node="4"><li><p data-path-to-node="4,0,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="4,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">Web/Browser:</b> <code data-path-to-node="4,0,0" data-index-in-node="13">Fetch API</code>, <code data-path-to-node="4,0,0" data-index-in-node="24">XMLHttpRequest</code>, <code data-path-to-node="4,0,0" data-index-in-node="40">WebSockets</code>, <code data-path-to-node="4,0,0" data-index-in-node="52">WebRTC</code> (Real-time communication).</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="4,1,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="4,1,0" data-index-in-node="0">Node.js/Runtime:</b> <code data-path-to-node="4,1,0" data-index-in-node="17">http</code>, <code data-path-to-node="4,1,0" data-index-in-node="23">https</code>, <code data-path-to-node="4,1,0" data-index-in-node="30">net</code> (TCP streams), <code data-path-to-node="4,1,0" data-index-in-node="49">dgram</code> (UDP), <code data-path-to-node="4,1,0" data-index-in-node="62">dns</code>.</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="4,2,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="4,2,0" data-index-in-node="0">Internal Working:</b> Yeh niche baithe <b data-path-to-node="4,2,0" data-index-in-node="35">Network Stack</b> (Chromium Stack/Necko) ka use karti hain jo OS ke <b data-path-to-node="4,2,0" data-index-in-node="99">Socket layer</b> se baat karta hai.</font></p></li></ul><h3 data-path-to-node="5"><font style="font-size: 72px;">2. File System &amp; Storage APIs</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="6"><font style="font-size: 72px;">Memory aur Disk par data read/write karne wali APIs.</font></p><ul data-path-to-node="7"><li><p data-path-to-node="7,0,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="7,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">Web/Browser:</b> <code data-path-to-node="7,0,0" data-index-in-node="13">IndexedDB</code> (NoSQL database), <code data-path-to-node="7,0,0" data-index-in-node="41">LocalStorage</code>, <code data-path-to-node="7,0,0" data-index-in-node="55">Cookie Store API</code>, <code data-path-to-node="7,0,0" data-index-in-node="73">File System Access API</code> (Modern browsers).</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="7,1,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="7,1,0" data-index-in-node="0">Node.js/Runtime:</b> <code data-path-to-node="7,1,0" data-index-in-node="17">fs</code> (File System), <code data-path-to-node="7,1,0" data-index-in-node="35">path</code>, <code data-path-to-node="7,1,0" data-index-in-node="41">readline</code>.</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="7,2,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="7,2,0" data-index-in-node="0">Internal Working:</b> Yeh APIs <b data-path-to-node="7,2,0" data-index-in-node="27">Libuv</b> ya <b data-path-to-node="7,2,0" data-index-in-node="36">Tokio</b> ke thread pool ka use karti hain taaki heavy disk I/O main thread ko freeze na kare.</font></p></li></ul><h3 data-path-to-node="8"><font style="font-size: 72px;">3. System &amp; Hardware Interaction APIs</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="9"><font style="font-size: 72px;">Jo directly computer ke hardware elements (CPU, GPU, RAM, Sensors) se interact karti hain.</font></p><ul data-path-to-node="10"><li><p data-path-to-node="10,0,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="10,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">Web/Browser:</b> <code data-path-to-node="10,0,0" data-index-in-node="13">Canvas API</code> (GPU rendering), <code data-path-to-node="10,0,0" data-index-in-node="41">Web Audio API</code>, <code data-path-to-node="10,0,0" data-index-in-node="56">Geolocation</code>, <code data-path-to-node="10,0,0" data-index-in-node="69">Web Bluetooth</code>, <code data-path-to-node="10,0,0" data-index-in-node="84">WebGPU</code>.</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="10,1,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="10,1,0" data-index-in-node="0">Node.js/Runtime:</b> <code data-path-to-node="10,1,0" data-index-in-node="17">os</code> (CPU/Memory info), <code data-path-to-node="10,1,0" data-index-in-node="39">crypto</code> (Hardware-level encryption), <code data-path-to-node="10,1,0" data-index-in-node="75">worker_threads</code> (CPU parallelism), <code data-path-to-node="10,1,0" data-index-in-node="109">buffer</code> (Direct memory access).</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="10,2,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="10,2,0" data-index-in-node="0">Internal Working:</b> Yeh wo layer hai jahan <b data-path-to-node="10,2,0" data-index-in-node="41">RSP/RBP registers</b> ka context sabse zyada active hota hai kyunki hardware level par instructions process ho rahi hoti hain.</font></p></li></ul><h3 data-path-to-node="11"><font style="font-size: 72px;">4. Special Binary/Memory APIs</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="12"><font style="font-size: 72px;">JS aur Native code ke beech data transfer ko fast banane ke liye:</font></p><ul data-path-to-node="13"><li><p data-path-to-node="13,0,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="13,0,0" data-index-in-node="0">Common:</b> <code data-path-to-node="13,0,0" data-index-in-node="8">ArrayBuffer</code>, <code data-path-to-node="13,0,0" data-index-in-node="21">SharedArrayBuffer</code>, <code data-path-to-node="13,0,0" data-index-in-node="40">TypedArrays</code> (Uint8Array, etc.).</font></p></li><li><p data-path-to-node="13,1,0"><font style="font-size: 72px;"><b data-path-to-node="13,1,0" data-index-in-node="0">Note:</b> <b data-path-to-node="13,1,0" data-index-in-node="6">Bun</b> inhi ka use karke <b data-path-to-node="13,1,0" data-index-in-node="28">Zero-copy</b> achieve karta hai.</font></p></li></ul>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="3200" width="4780" x="53390" y="6690" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1014" edge="1" value="<h3 data-path-to-node="4"><font style="font-size: 100px;">SandBox Security Protocols:</font></h3><h3 data-path-to-node="4"><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">1. Same-Origin Policy (SOP)</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="5"><font style="font-size: 72px;">The foundational security mechanism for all modern browsers. It ensures scripts running on one origin (protocol, domain, and port) cannot read or modify resources from another origin. For example, <code dir="ltr" class="KDcb0c" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-processed="true" data-copy-service-computed-style="font-family: monospace; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; margin: 0px; text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1.11111px solid rgb(243, 245, 246);" style="margin: 0px; border-bottom: 1.11111px solid rgb(243, 245, 246);">https://site-A.com</code> cannot read the DOM or session data of <code dir="ltr" class="KDcb0c" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-processed="true" data-copy-service-computed-style="font-family: monospace; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; margin: 0px; text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1.11111px solid rgb(243, 245, 246);" style="margin: 0px; border-bottom: 1.11111px solid rgb(243, 245, 246);">https://site-B.com</code></font></p><ul data-path-to-node="6"><li><p data-path-to-node="6,0,0"><br></p></li></ul><h3 data-path-to-node="7"><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">2. Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS)</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="8"><font style="font-size: 72px;">Because strict SOP can be overly restrictive, CORS serves as an exception-handling mechanism. It is implemented via HTTP headers (like <code dir="ltr" class="KDcb0c" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-processed="true" data-copy-service-computed-style="font-family: monospace; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; margin: 0px; text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1.11111px solid rgb(243, 245, 246);" style="margin: 0px; border-bottom: 1.11111px solid rgb(243, 245, 246);">Access-Control-Allow-Origin</code>) sent by the server. It tells the browser which external domains are permitted to read the server's data.</font></p><h3 data-path-to-node="10"><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">3. Content Security Policy (CSP)</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="11"><font style="font-size: 72px;">A defense-in-depth mechanism that instructs the browser on which specific resources (like JavaScript, CSS, and Images) are allowed to load and execute. It mitigates Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks by restricting where scripts can be fetched from and preventing the execution of inline scripts.</font></p><ul data-path-to-node="12"><li><p data-path-to-node="12,0,0"><br></p></li></ul><h3 data-path-to-node="13"><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">4. Cross-Origin Opener Policy (COOP)</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="14"><font style="font-size: 72px;">A header-based mechanism that isolates your page from other documents that might open it (or that your page opens via <code dir="ltr" class="KDcb0c" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-processed="true" data-copy-service-computed-style="font-family: monospace; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; margin: 0px; text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1.11111px solid rgb(243, 245, 246);" style="margin: 0px; border-bottom: 1.11111px solid rgb(243, 245, 246);">window.open</code>). Setting COOP to <code dir="ltr" class="KDcb0c" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-processed="true" data-copy-service-computed-style="font-family: monospace; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; margin: 0px; text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1.11111px solid rgb(243, 245, 246);" style="margin: 0px; border-bottom: 1.11111px solid rgb(243, 245, 246);">same-origin</code> ensures your tab is placed in a separate browsing context group, preventing malicious cross-origin pop-ups from accessing your window's global object or leaking data.</font></p><h3 data-path-to-node="13"><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">5. Cross-Origin Embedder Policy (COEP)</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="14"><font style="font-size: 72px;"></font></p><p data-path-to-node="14"><font style="font-size: 72px;">Works closely with COOP and CORP to establish cross-origin isolation. When enabled, COEP prevents a document from loading any cross-origin resources (like iframes or images) unless that resource explicitly grants permission using CORS or Cross-Origin Resource Policy (CORP).</font></p><h3 data-path-to-node="13"><font style="font-size: 72px; color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">6. Sandbox (CSP &amp; iframe)</font></h3><p data-path-to-node="14"><font style="font-size: 72px;"></font></p><p data-path-to-node="14"><font style="font-size: 72px;"></font></p><p data-path-to-node="14"><font style="font-size: 72px;">A strict containment environment. When applied to an iframe (e.g., ) or via the <code dir="ltr" class="KDcb0c" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-processed="true" data-copy-service-computed-style="font-family: monospace; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; margin: 0px; text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1.11111px solid rgb(243, 245, 246);" style="margin: 0px; border-bottom: 1.11111px solid rgb(243, 245, 246);">Content-Security-Policy: sandbox</code> directive, the browser strips the embedded resource of potentially dangerous privileges. This includes preventing form submissions, plugin execution, script execution, and restricting access to unique origins</font></p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="2560" width="3900" x="29720" y="6490" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1327" connectable="0" parent="lXm1F2k2CTp0YzlQbwvI-1" value="<div><b style=""><font style="font-size: 48px; background-color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">3. Background / Worker Threads</font></b></div><div><b><font style="font-size: 36px;">(Inside Renderer Process)&nbsp;</font></b></div><div><span style="font-size: 36px; background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));">Browsers spin off additional helper threads&nbsp;</span><span style="font-size: 36px; background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));">to perform intensive operations without&nbsp;</span><span style="font-size: 36px; background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));">blocking the main thread or UI rendering.</span></div><div><b><font style="font-size: 36px; background-color: rgb(0, 102, 204);">• Web Worker Threads:</font></b></div><div><font style="font-size: 36px;"><b>&nbsp;</b>Run heavy JavaScript computations in&nbsp;</font><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));"><font style="font-size: 36px;">separate threads with their own event loop,&nbsp;</font></span><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));"><font style="font-size: 36px;">call stack, and heap.</font></span></div><div><b><font style="font-size: 36px; background-color: rgb(0, 0, 153);">• Service Worker Threads:</font></b></div><div><font style="font-size: 36px;"><b>&nbsp; </b>Handle network interception, offline caching,&nbsp;</font><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));"><font style="font-size: 36px;">background sync, and push notifications&nbsp;</font></span><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));"><font style="font-size: 36px;">independently of page lifecycle.</font></span></div><div><b><font style="font-size: 36px; background-color: rgb(51, 0, 102);">• Audio / Video Threads:</font></b></div><div><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));"><font style="font-size: 36px;">Decode, process, and synchronize media</font></span><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));"><font style="font-size: 36px;">&nbsp;streams to ensure smooth playback.</font></span></div><div><b><font style="font-size: 36px; background-color: rgb(102, 0, 102);">• Concurrent / Parallel GC Threads:</font></b></div><div><font style="font-size: 36px;"><b>&nbsp;</b> Assist garbage collection by performing&nbsp;</font><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));"><font style="font-size: 36px;">marking, sweeping, and memory-management&nbsp;</font></span><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));"><font style="font-size: 36px;">work in the background, reducing pauses&nbsp;</font></span><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));"><font style="font-size: 36px;">on the main JavaScript thread.</font></span></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="670" width="1600" x="20" y="1610" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="KBbk7kHsDggTW9L0QgLO-8" parent="KBbk7kHsDggTW9L0QgLO-111" style="shape=doubleArrow;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;gradientColor=#66FFFF;fillColor=#FF99CC;gradientDirection=east;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="<font style="font-size: 72px;"><b>Host+Runtime Shared Services</b></font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="369.52" width="1173.74" x="4346.260000000002" y="4920" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="KBbk7kHsDggTW9L0QgLO-88" connectable="0" parent="KBbk7kHsDggTW9L0QgLO-111" style="group;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="2070" width="1680" x="1100" y="1120" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="KBbk7kHsDggTW9L0QgLO-89" parent="KBbk7kHsDggTW9L0QgLO-88" style="shape=ext;double=1;rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#4C0099;fontStyle=4;container=0;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="1511.33" width="1480" x="110" y="58.67" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="KBbk7kHsDggTW9L0QgLO-90" parent="KBbk7kHsDggTW9L0QgLO-88" style="shape=ext;double=1;rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#FF99CC;fontStyle=4;container=0;movable=1;resizable=1;rotatable=1;deletable=1;editable=1;locked=0;connectable=1;" value="<span style="color: rgb(26, 28, 30); current Lexical Env's(GLE/FLE), following the scope chain until it finds the binding or reaches 'null'. Example- x=

switch.text: RunTime Bindings --->In 'use strict' It is a Methode, Instruction and step by step live lookup process where the engine resolves identifiers at execution time by searching through the Env Records(OER/DER/FER) within the current Lexical Env's(GLE/FLE), following the scope chain until it finds the binding or reaches 'null'. Example- x=

p.span: This is the "Physical Sandbox" that is unique to each individual tab
. It contains the following elements:

ul.style: padding-inline-start: 32px; font-family: "Google Sans Text", sans-serif !important; line-height: 1.15 !important; margin-top: 0px !important;

p.span: Rendering Engine: Its job is to parse HTML and CSS to display pixels on the screen
. (For example, Blink in Chrome and WebKit in Safari)
JavaScript Engine(The Core): The "brain" that executes code (such as the V8 Engine)
. It consists of components like the Ignition Interpreter and TurboFan Compiler
Web APIs: Features provided by the browser, such as the DOM API, Fetch API, Canvas, and Console
Web Workers Threads: Logical threads that perform heavy calculations in the background to keep the main thread free

p.span: There is only one network process for the entire browser, and the Network Engine is a part of it

ul.style: padding-inline-start: 32px; font-family: "Google Sans Text", sans-serif !important; line-height: 1.15 !important; margin-top: 0px !important;

p.span: Network Engine: This is the actual "Messenger" that fetches data from the internet (Server)
. It handles HTTP/HTTPS protocols and sockets
Service Worker: This acts as a "Network Proxy" that sits between the Network Process and the Renderer to intercept requests (for PWA features)

p.span: Web Storage: LocalStorage and SessionStorage (used for saving small amounts of data)
IndexedDB: A client-side NoSQL database for heavy data and complex structures
Cache API: Used by the Service Worker to save files for offline use

p.span: Even without a browser, the engine remains the heart of the runtime
Role: Compiles and executes JavaScript code into machine instructions
Components: For Node/Deno, it is V8 (featuring the Ignition interpreter and TurboFan compiler); for Bun, it is JavaScriptCore

h3.b: 2. Runtime APIs / Native Bindings (The OS Bridge)

p.span: Since there is no window or document object, the runtime provides APIs to interact with the server's environment
File System (fs): Allows reading, writing, and managing files on the hard drive
Network Stack (http/https/net): Enables the creation of web servers and handling of TCP/UDP sockets
Process Object: Provides information about the current running process and control over the environment (the server-side equivalent to window)
Crypto & OS APIs: For high-level security/encryption and system-level information (CPU, RAM usage)

switch.text: 2. Runtime APIs / Native Bindings (The OS Bridge)...

p.span: This layer allows JavaScript to communicate with low-level C, C++, or Rust code
N-API (Node-API): Provides a stable interface for C++ addons to ensure they don't break when the JS engine updates
FFI (Foreign Function Interface): Used heavily in Bun (Bun-ffi) to call native functions with near-zero overhead

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 2538px; height: 1px; padding-top: 25125px; margin-left: 11871px;

h3.b: 3. Event Loop & Thread Pool (The Asynchronous Orchestrator)

p.span: Server runtimes must handle thousands of concurrent connections without blocking the main execution thread
Event Loop: Continuously monitors the call stack and task queues to manage asynchronous execution
Libuv (Node.js) / Tokio (Deno/Bun): A C++ or Rust library that provides a Thread Pool to handle "heavy" or "blocking" tasks like file I/O or DNS lookups in the background

switch.text: 3. Event Loop & Thread Pool (The Asynchronous Orchestrator)...

div.font: 1.Concurrent Marking:GC Healper thread can perform marking tasks in background, while the Main thread continues executing JS.
2.Parallel Sweeping: When its time to reclaim memory, the Engine uses multiple helper threads to work in parallel with the Main thread(Only in Parallel Sweeping) to speedup the process.

font.font: Those codes Loops, func() or func(loop) which calls, loops(not untile loop end) & run multiple times. Interpreter generates byte code and side by side counting in backgound by Profiling counter & also detects in loop's internal code(On-Stack Replacement-OSR). It's also used in Hidden Class inline Caching.

"Hot Code detection is the 'Brain' of V8. By using Profiling counters and OSR, the V8::platform TaskScheduler identifies repetitive paths and offloads them to background compiler threads (V8 pick Hot Code from RAM & put into the CPU Registers). This ensures the main thread is never blocked while the JIT pipeline transforms slow Bytecode into high-performance Machine Code."

font.b: "V8 maps JS variables to CPU Registers(CPU's internal registers) for near-instant calculations(using ALU) & also holds the result(in ALU registers)."

div.font: CPU Register for Pointer Management: Its holds the CallStack's and HEAP's address in special registers.
1. Register Stack Pointer(RSP): It tells which func() is running[current execution context]
2. Register Base Pointer(RBP): It track the starting points of func() variables. First RSP after execution stored as RBP
Without RSP&RBP registers JS can't know where GEC ends and from where FEC starts.
ALU Rgister(RAX): It's hold func(){return value} and ALU OP results.

font.font: It's holds the func(){return} value & ALU's operational result.

font.b: CPU Internal Registers= General Purpose Registers(Takes Data from RAM & Holds in Variable Registers)+ALU Registers(RAX)(Holds ALU's compute Result)+Control Registers(RSP+RBP)

switch.text: "V8 maps JS variables to CPU Registers(CPU's internal registers) for near-instant calculations(using ALU) & also holds the result(in ALU registers)."...

div.font: "A thread is the smallest unit of execution
within a process. It represents a sequence
of instructions that can be managed
├─ User-Level / Logical/ Software Threads (Managed by runtime)
├─ Kernel-Level/ Native/ OS Threads (Managed by OS)

div.span: It is a carrier to run on CPU.
i. M:N Mapping: Map "M" no. of logical threads on "N" no. of Native(Kernal) Threads.
ii.Worker Threads: Node.js , Bun, Deno makes "worker logical thread" which uses runTime native threadPool.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 4378px; height: 1px; padding-top: 6815px; margin-left: 21341px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

p.font: The most significant element of a logical thread is its State. When a thread is paused (during a context switch), its current state is saved.
Instruction Pointer: This keeps track of how much code the Logical Thread has executed.

Registers State: Even though physical registers exist within the CPU, every logical thread keeps a "Copy" (Snapshot) of its registers in memory. This ensures that when it returns, it can resume exactly from where it left off.
Every logical thread has its own virtual memory segment called the Stack.
Local Variables: Temporary variables created inside functions.
Function Call History: A record of which function called whom (Call Stack).
Logic: Since this is at a logical level, the size of this stack is decided by the runtime (Node/Bun/Deno), such as a default of 1MB.
Since a single process can have thousands of logical threads, certain elements are necessary to manage them:
Priority Level: Determines which thread is more important (e.g., UI thread vs. Background thread).
Thread ID (LID): A logical identity used by the runtime (which may differ from the Native TID).
Status: Defines the current phase (Ready, Running, Blocked, Waiting).
A logical thread cannot run on hardware by itself; it requires a Carrier.
M:N Mapping: This refers to mapping "M" number of Logical Threads onto "N" number of Native (Kernel) Threads.
Worker Threads: In Node.js or Bun, when you create a "Worker," it acts as a logical thread that utilizes the runtime's native thread pool.

p.font: Yeh APIs external servers aur devices se data exchange karne ka kaam karti hain.
Web/Browser: Fetch API, XMLHttpRequest, WebSockets, WebRTC (Real-time communication).
Node.js/Runtime: http, https, net (TCP streams), dgram (UDP), dns.
Internal Working: Yeh niche baithe Network Stack (Chromium Stack/Necko) ka use karti hain jo OS ke Socket layer se baat karta hai.
Memory aur Disk par data read/write karne wali APIs.
Web/Browser: IndexedDB (NoSQL database), LocalStorage, Cookie Store API, File System Access API (Modern browsers).
Node.js/Runtime: fs (File System), path, readline.
Internal Working: Yeh APIs Libuv ya Tokio ke thread pool ka use karti hain taaki heavy disk I/O main thread ko freeze na kare.
Jo directly computer ke hardware elements (CPU, GPU, RAM, Sensors) se interact karti hain.
Web/Browser: Canvas API (GPU rendering), Web Audio API, Geolocation, Web Bluetooth, WebGPU.
Node.js/Runtime: os (CPU/Memory info), crypto (Hardware-level encryption), worker_threads (CPU parallelism), buffer (Direct memory access).
Internal Working: Yeh wo layer hai jahan RSP/RBP registers ka context sabse zyada active hota hai kyunki hardware level par instructions process ho rahi hoti hain.
JS aur Native code ke beech data transfer ko fast banane ke liye:
Common: ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, TypedArrays (Uint8Array, etc.).
Note: Bun inhi ka use karke Zero-copy achieve karta hai.

p.font: The foundational security mechanism for all modern browsers. It ensures scripts running on one origin (protocol, domain, and port) cannot read or modify resources from another origin. For example, https://site-A.com cannot read the DOM or session data of https://site-B.com

Because strict SOP can be overly restrictive, CORS serves as an exception-handling mechanism. It is implemented via HTTP headers (like Access-Control-Allow-Origin) sent by the server. It tells the browser which external domains are permitted to read the server's data.

A defense-in-depth mechanism that instructs the browser on which specific resources (like JavaScript, CSS, and Images) are allowed to load and execute. It mitigates Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks by restricting where scripts can be fetched from and preventing the execution of inline scripts.

A header-based mechanism that isolates your page from other documents that might open it (or that your page opens via window.open). Setting COOP to same-origin ensures your tab is placed in a separate browsing context group, preventing malicious cross-origin pop-ups from accessing your window's global object or leaking data.

Works closely with COOP and CORP to establish cross-origin isolation. When enabled, COEP prevents a document from loading any cross-origin resources (like iframes or images) unless that resource explicitly grants permission using CORS or Cross-Origin Resource Policy (CORP).

A strict containment environment. When applied to an iframe (e.g., ) or via the Content-Security-Policy: sandbox directive, the browser strips the embedded resource of potentially dangerous privileges. This includes preventing form submissions, plugin execution, script execution, and restricting access to unique origins
